hero_sub: The definitive Vocaloid rhythm game experience — where music, motion, and Miku collide.
video_no_support: Your browser does not support the video element.
about_text: Project DIVA is a critically acclaimed rhythm game series co-developed by SEGA and Crypton Future Media. Players tap, hold, and swipe notes in perfect sync with iconic Vocaloid tracks, unlocking hundreds of costumes and experiencing richly animated music videos. From its 2009 PSP debut to the 2022 PC release of MegaMix+, Project DIVA has defined a generation of music games.
chars_sub: Hover over a card to learn more — or visit the Characters page for full profiles.
preview_hint: Hover over an image below to preview it here.
chars_page_sub: Six voices. Infinite songs. Hover a card to reveal each character's full profile — or tap to flip on mobile.
skin_sub: Hundreds of unlockable costumes and five legendary tracks that define the Project DIVA experience.
gallery_sub: Hover over a cover to preview — click to visit the full module list for that version.
skin_spotlight_sub: Iconic modules from Hatsune Miku: Project DIVA MegaMix+
skin_gallery_cta: Want to explore all modules? Browse the full gallery ↓

songs_sub: Five legendary tracks that shaped the Project DIVA series — and Vocaloid music as a whole.
ver_sub: From a humble PSP debut to a Nintendo Switch global release — 15 years of rhythm game evolution across five major titles.
gameplay_sub: Three iconic songs — three levels of challenge. See how the chart design evolves from beginner-friendly to brutally expert.
miku_desc: CV01 — The world's most iconic virtual singer. Miku's teal twin-tails and optimistic soprano voice have defined Vocaloid music since her 2007 debut.
rin_desc: CV02 — Energetic and fierce, Rin's bright soprano cuts through any genre. From ballads to metal, her versatility has made her a fan favourite since 2007.
len_desc: CV02 — Rin's twin brother with a warmer, lighter tenor. Len's songs span dramatic epics and comedic storytelling, always delivered with unmistakable flair.
luka_desc: CV03 — Bilingual, mature, and effortlessly cool. Luka's deep mezzo-soprano is the voice of sophisticated Vocaloid music, equally at home in jazz and EDM.
kaito_desc: The first Japanese male Vocaloid — KAITO's warm, resonant baritone and signature blue scarf have made him an enduring symbol of the entire Vocaloid movement since 2006.
meiko_desc: The first Japanese female Vocaloid — MEIKO's bold, expressive alto voice and red design set the foundation for all of Crypton's future creations, debuting in 2004.
teto_desc: UTAU · Guest character in MegaMix+ — Kasane Teto is the most popular UTAU voicebank, originally created as an April Fools' joke. Her cheerful personality and iconic drill twin-tails have made her an honorary Vocaloid icon.
neru_desc: Derivative · Guest in MegaMix+ — Akita Neru is an unofficial Hatsune Miku derivative known for her rebellious attitude and phone addiction. Her yellow-black color scheme and side ponytail are instantly recognizable in the community.
haku_desc: Derivative · Guest in MegaMix+ — Yowane Haku represents the melancholic side of Vocaloid culture — a derivative created to embody failed or mediocre Vocaloid usage. Her silver hair and quiet demeanor have made her unexpectedly beloved.
producers_sub: The composers and arrangers whose songs power Project DIVA. These artists defined the sound of a generation.
producers_hint: Hover or tap cards for notable Project DIVA tracks.
concerts_sub: From the first Magical Mirai to worldwide Miku Expo tours — the biggest stages in Vocaloid history.
gallery_wiki_alt: Project DIVA official Fandom Wiki homepage
gallery_megamix_alt: Hatsune Miku Project DIVA Mega Mix Plus official key visual
gallery_ft_alt: Project DIVA Arcade Future Tone key visual
gallery_f_alt: Hatsune Miku Project DIVA F promotional art
gallery_2nd_alt: Hatsune Miku Project DIVA 2nd PSP game cover
gallery_mm_alt: Project DIVA MegaMix Nintendo Switch gameplay
song_1_desc: One of the most streamed Vocaloid songs of all time. Miku channels a lovable, self-proclaimed queen demanding affection. Its bouncy synth-pop arrangement and relatable lyrics define an era.
song_2_desc: Originally a Google Chrome advertisement, this sweeping synth-pop anthem grew into one of Miku's most emotionally resonant songs, celebrating creativity and global connection.
song_3_desc: A relentless fusion of shamisen motifs and driving EDM that evokes Meiji-era Japan. One of the most technically demanding charts in the series and a staple of Miku's live concerts worldwide.
song_4_desc: A technically brutal track infamous for its extreme BPM and near-impossible-to-follow lyrics. A narrative poem about Miku's identity and obsolescence — a landmark in Vocaloid storytelling.
song_5_desc: The tender ballad that launched Vocaloid into the mainstream. Miku narrates the nervous flutter of a first crush with warm synth pads and a melody so memorable it remains ryo's most beloved work.
ver_2009_body: The PSP release that started it all. 38 songs, a pioneering note-tap system, and the first time players could dress Miku in custom modules. It launched a movement and proved Vocaloid music had a dedicated gaming audience.
ver_2010_body: The PSP sequel introduced all six Crypton Vocaloids as playable characters for the first time, expanded the setlist significantly, and refined the chart design philosophy that subsequent entries would build on.
ver_2012_body: A landmark leap to home console and handheld HD hardware. The debut of the beloved "Diva Room" social mode and touchscreen scratch notes elevated the series. Sharp HD visuals made music videos genuinely cinematic.
ver_2016_body: Introduced a story-driven "Live Quest" campaign across five themed cloud worlds. Players collected voltage by performing well to unlock new songs and modules — a structural experiment that deepened the series beyond pure score-chasing.
ver_2020_body: The Nintendo Switch debut brought dual-mode gameplay: MegaMix touch mode alongside the classic button-press mode. MegaMix+ later brought the full 238-song arcade catalog to PC via Steam — Miku's first true home on every major platform.
gp_1_desc: A welcoming chart with steady 4/4 rhythm and well-telegraphed note patterns. The ideal entry point for new players — accessible enough to learn the basics, satisfying enough to replay indefinitely.
gp_2_desc: A mid-to-high difficulty chart demanding consistent double-tap sequences matching the shamisen arpeggios. Swipe gestures on the chorus separate good players from great ones — muscle memory is essential.
gp_3_desc: One of the most technically demanding charts in the entire catalog. Rapid multi-button holds, dense sixteenth-note streams, and sudden BPM variations make a Perfect run an achievement worth celebrating.
gp_3_caption: The Intense Voice of Hatsune Miku — Extreme Chart
prod_ryo_desc: The composer behind Miku's breakout global hits. His bright, anthemic style defined the early DIVA era and remains a benchmark for chart difficulty and emotional delivery.
prod_wowaka_desc: Iconic for raw, intense rock tracks with complex emotional narratives. His work pushed the technical and storytelling boundaries of Vocaloid music in DIVA.
prod_cosmo_desc: Master of extreme BPM and technical showcases. The Disappearance... became legendary for its near-impossible charts and remains a rite of passage for advanced DIVA players.
prod_kz_desc: Created one of Miku's most uplifting anthems originally for Google Chrome. His clean, soaring synth-pop translates beautifully into cinematic DIVA PVs.
prod_kurousa_desc: Fused shamisen and Edo-period aesthetics with driving modern EDM. Senbonzakura's chart is one of the most beloved and technically demanding in the entire series.
prod_crusher_desc: Pioneer of high-quality English Vocaloid music that found its way into DIVA libraries. Brings Western pop production values and powerful vocal chops to the franchise.

gameHistory.title: Series History
gameHistory.subtitle: Fifteen years of rhythm. From a 2009 PSP experiment to a global PC phenomenon — every mainline Project DIVA title, documented.
gameHistory.filterAria: Filter games by platform or series
gameHistory.footerCredit: Release data compiled from official SEGA announcements and franchise archives.

concert_1_desc: The inaugural Magical Mirai. A landmark two-day event that established the template for all future Miku hologram concerts: massive 3D projection, live band, and an ecstatic sea of glow sticks.
concert_1_highlight: First official major live celebration of both Miku and the Project DIVA series.
concert_2_desc: Miku's explosive first dedicated tour of North America. Sold-out arenas proved Western fans were ready for full-scale Vocaloid live experiences.
concert_2_highlight: Brought arcade-authentic production and DIVA song selections to thousands of new international fans.
concert_3_desc: The premier yearly event. Each edition features brand-new 3D Miku models, surprise guests, and setlists that heavily feature recent Project DIVA additions.
concert_3_highlight: Has been running annually since 2013. Consistently sells out within minutes; the spiritual home of the DIVA community.
concert_4_desc: Expanded the Expo format globally with stops in London, Paris, Jakarta, and more. Featured many MegaMix-era tracks and new modules.
concert_4_highlight: Further globalized the franchise and influenced later DLC song selections.
concert_5_desc: First major post-pandemic tour. Celebrated the arrival of MegaMix+ on PC with heavy representation of 238-song library tracks.
concert_5_highlight: Largest production values yet; many songs directly pulled from recent DIVA titles.
skin_miku_infinity_desc: A sleek, futuristic outfit built around the infinity symbol — Miku in sharp monochrome with glowing accents. Minimal yet striking, it embodies the timeless, boundless nature of her voice and appeal.
skin_miku_sakura1_desc: A striking Taisho-era inspired military uniform in muted cherry-bark tones. Complete with a matching cap and elegant flowing sleeves, this module perfectly captures the anachronistic, historical-meets-modern vibe of the national hit, Senbonzakura
skin_miku_sakura2_desc: The iconic spring variant of Miku. Retaining her classic V2 silhouette but reimagined in vibrant pinks with cherry blossom hair accessories, this limited-edition design brings a fresh, seasonal warmth to her signature look.
skin_rin_cheerful_candy_desc: Bright, sweet, and bursting with color — Rin in a candy-themed outfit that perfectly matches her energetic, mischievous personality. A crowd favourite for how well it captures her cheerful core identity.
skin_rin_cheerful_desc: Rin at her most vibrant — a cheerleader-inspired design with bold yellows and dynamic proportions. Fans love this module for how it amplifies her high-energy presence during uptempo charts.
skin_rin_sakura2_desc: A uniquely serene module for Rin, featuring a beige and brown color palette with delicate butterfly motifs. The standout feature is her elegant, draped hood, offering a gentle, vintage aesthetic that contrasts beautifully with her usual energetic style.
skin_len_punkish_desc: A rebellious, edgy take on Len. Featuring an asymmetrical sleeveless top, shorts, and thigh-high boots, this standout module proves Len can effortlessly carry an aggressive, hard-rock aesthetic without losing his core identity.
skin_len_bluemoon_desc: A sophisticated, mature visual identity for Len. Swapping his typical futuristic gear for a sharp white shirt, black vest, and his signature yellow tie, Blue Moon offers a sleek, handsome look designed to complement his mature persona.
skin_len_eraser_desc: Characterized by its brilliant white palette, elegant gold accents, and striking half-cape. This module perfectly embodies the sci-fi idol theme of his iconic duet, offering Len a sharp and mature aesthetic.
skin_luka_blossom_desc: Luka in a flowing pink and white stage dress with cherry blossom accents — feminine, graceful, and perfectly matched to her cool mezzo-soprano. One of her most elegant modules in the entire series.
skin_luka_chiffon_dress_desc: A hauntingly minimalist design — a flowing pale chiffon dress with asymmetric layering that drifts beautifully in motion. Luka's most emotionally understated module, perfectly suited to the melancholic isolation of Double Lariat.
skin_luka_cybernation_desc: Luka fully embracing a sleek cyberpunk identity — sharp lines, dark bodysuit with electric pink circuit accents, and an aura of effortless cool. Bilingual, mature, and unmistakably futuristic.
skin_kaito_diamond_dust_desc: KAITO in a pristine white winter formal with crystalline ice-blue accents that catch the light like falling snow. Clean, refined, and quietly majestic — the definitive expression of his cool, resonant baritone persona.
skin_kaito_originator_desc: A bold reimagining of KAITO's classic silhouette — deep navy with structured shoulders and gold trim that nods to his status as the first Japanese male Vocaloid. Authoritative, timeless, and unmistakably him.
skin_kaito_requiem_desc: KAITO's darkest and most dramatic module — a baroque-inspired black and deep wine costume with gold brocade and a sweeping half-cape. Every detail embodies the seductive, theatrical tension of Cantarella.
skin_meiko_blue_crystal_desc: A striking departure from MEIKO's signature red — a structured cobalt and crystal-white stage outfit with sharp geometric accents. Sophisticated and commanding, it proves MEIKO dominates any palette she touches.
skin_meiko_sakura_camellia_desc: The boldest of the Senbonzakura trio — MEIKO in a fiery red and gold military uniform with dramatic epaulettes and flowing camellia motifs. Power, authority, and unmistakable MEIKO energy distilled into a single costume.
skin_meiko_scarlet_desc: MEIKO leaning fully into her rock vocalist identity — a leather jacket, high boots, and her signature crimson palette pushed to maximum saturation. Raw, unapologetic, and perfectly matched to her bold alto voice.
skin_teto_default_desc: Teto's iconic classic look — the red military uniform and matching drill twin-tails that made her an instant fan favourite. The starting point for everything, and still the most recognisable.
skin_teto_msj_desc: A sharp, modernised stage jacket in deep crimson with silver detailing and structured shoulders — Teto's drill twin-tails framed perfectly by the high collar. Authoritative yet playful, exactly as she'd want.
skin_teto_swimwear_desc: Teto fully off-duty — a bright pink and white swimsuit that keeps her characteristic cheerfulness intact. Her drill twin-tails refuse to be tamed even at the beach. Carefree energy at maximum.
skin_neru_aborigine_desc: A wildly eclectic mix of tribal patterns and Neru's signature yellow-black palette — chaotic in the best possible way. The culture clash aesthetic mirrors her chaotic, rebellious personality perfectly.
skin_neru_school_club_desc: Neru reluctantly in uniform — a yellow and black school outfit she's already halfway through customising with attitude. Phone still in hand, rules still optional. Her most relatable look by a significant margin.
skin_neru_swimwear_desc: Even at the beach Neru looks like she'd rather be texting. Yellow and black bikini, side ponytail immaculate, phone waterproofed and ready. Effortlessly stylish without trying in the slightest.
skin_haku_cyber_dive_desc: Haku rendered in a sleek cyberpunk aesthetic — dark bodysuit with pale blue circuit traces and a visor that half-obscures her melancholic expression. A quietly unsettling contrast between cold technology and soft sorrow.
skin_haku_gothic_purple_desc: Haku in a dramatic deep violet gothic ballgown with black lace trim and wilting floral details. Her silver hair and quiet demeanour make the gothic aesthetic feel completely natural — like it was always her true form.
skin_haku_swimwear_desc: Haku at the beach — pale, slightly lost, and somehow still melancholic in a white and lavender swimsuit. Her silver hair catches the light beautifully. She's trying to have fun. It's going okay.
